zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan la Warre於大約1364年出生在英國時，她的父親Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr39歲，母親Eleanor De Mowbray36歲。她於1384年5月24日與Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1404年4月24日在英國素可艾欽加姆享年41歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素可艾欽加姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan la Warre于大约1364年出生在英国时，她的父亲Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr39岁，母亲Eleanor De Mowbray36岁。她于1384年5月24日与Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West在英国戴文结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1404年4月24日在英国素可艾欽加姆享年41岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素可艾欽加姆。

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Warre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Warr
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname for a warrior, usually from Norman French were, werre, waire, warre (central French guerre) ‘war, battle’, and short for de la werre (de la guerre, see Delaware ), literally ‘(man) of the war’, a construction paralleled in de la Bataille (see Battle ). Occasionally it is a shortened form of Old French werreur ‘warrior’.

variants.name: Ware
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by or looked after a dam or weir on a river (from Old English wær, wer, Middle English war(e), warre, wer(e), werre ‘weir’), or a habitational name from a place called with this word, such as Weare (Somerset), Weare Giffard (Devon), or Ware (Hertfordshire).
topographic name for someone who lived by a horse pond or fish pond, from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English wayour, which became early modern English ware, wear.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan la Warre was born about 1364, in England, her father, Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr, was 39 and her mother, Eleanor De Mowbray, was 36. She married Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West on 24 May 1384, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 24 April 1404, in Etchingham, Sussex, England, at the age of 41, and was buried in Etchingham, Sussex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan la Warre est née environ 1364, à Angleterre, son père, Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Eleanor De Mowbray, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West le 24 mai 1384, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 24 avril 1404, à Etchingham, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterrée à Etchingham, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan la Warre um 1364 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr 39 und ihre Mutter Eleanor De Mowbray 36. Sie hat am 24. Mai 1384 in Devon, England Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 24. April 1404 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Etchingham, Sussex, England und wurde in Etchingham, Sussex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan la Warre è nata il circa 1364 nel England, sua padre, Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Eleanor De Mowbray, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West il 24 maggio 1384, nel Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 24 aprile 1404, nel Etchingham, Sussex, England, all'età di 41 anni, e fu sepolta a Etchingham, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan la Warreが約1364年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warrは39歳，母Eleanor De Mowbrayは36歳であった。1384年5月24日にイギリスデボンでSir Thomas West 1st Baron Westと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1404年4月24日にイギリススセックスエトチンガムで41歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススセックスエトチンガムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan la Warre(이)가 대략 1364년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor De Mowbray(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1384년 5월 24일 영국 데폰에서 Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1404년 4월 24일 영국 수세스 트친그함에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 트친그함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan la Warre nasceu aproximadamente 1364, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor De Mowbray, tinha 36 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West em 24 de maio de 1384, em Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 24 de abril de 1404, em Etchingham, Sussex, Inglaterra, com 41 anos, e foi sepultada em Etchingham, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan la Warre родилась около 1364 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor De Mowbray, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West 24 мая 1384 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 24 апреля 1404 г. в местности эчингхам, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 41 год и была похоронена в местности эчингхам, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan la Warre nació aproximadamente 1364, en Inglaterra, su papá, Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Eleanor De Mowbray, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas West 1st Baron West el 24 de mayo de 1384, en Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 24 de abril de 1404, en Etchingham, Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultada en Etchingham, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
